Title: Hardcoded Center password in Edge v2 JWT login
Evidence:
json={"username":"admin","password":"...", Leaked source code exposes Center admin credentials,Move to env var EDGE_CENTER_PASSWORD with no default,Backend,2h,P0,SOURCE_FIXED
SEC-002,7 non-standard ports publicly exposed,CRITICAL,CWE-200,VPS Network,VPS iptables/ufw,-,ss -lntup shows 9998 9999 4840 4841 7000 8002 8100 on 0.0.0.0"
ExploitScenario: Direct access to simulator test server OPC UA and unknown services from internet
Remediation: Close all ports except 80+22 via ufw. Route Edge v2 via nginx reverse proxy

Title: No HTTPS on public-facing services
Evidence: curl -v shows HTTP only no redirect
ExploitScenario: Man-in-the-middle captures JWT tokens session cookies and passwords
Remediation: Add Let's Encrypt TLS to nginx. Redirect HTTP→HTTPS. Set Secure flag on cookies

Title: Default session_secret in Edge v2 config
ExploitScenario: Predictable session secret allows session forgery across all Edge v2 users
Remediation: Generate random 64-char secret. Move to EDGE_SESSION_SECRET env var. Remove from YAML

Title: Default API key in deployment
ExploitScenario: Default key grants Edge API access if env var not set
Remediation: Generate random key. Remove default value from compose file. Document rotation

Title: Public Edge v2 without TLS
Evidence: Edge v2 login page served over HTTP on public IP
ExploitScenario: Session cookies transmitted in cleartext over internet
Remediation: Route Edge v2 through nginx with TLS OR restrict to localhost only

Title: Password hashes exported without TLS
Evidence: Returns password_hash field in JSON response
ExploitScenario: Hashes captured in transit enable offline cracking
Remediation: Add TLS for Center→Edge sync. Consider service-scoped API key. Add audit log

Title: DOM XSS via innerHTML in nav.js
ExploitScenario: Malicious connector name or label injected via config could execute JS
Remediation: Use textContent or DOMPurify for dynamic content

Title: EMQX unhealthy
ExploitScenario: Message broker failure could impact MQTT-dependent features. Unknown security patch status
Remediation: Fix or remove EMQX from deployment

Title: No rate limiting on login
ExploitScenario: Brute-force attack on admin password possible
Remediation: Add rate limiting middleware (e.g. slowapi) to login endpoints

Title: ThresholdConfig type missing after refactor
ExploitScenario: Build fails blocking deployment
Remediation: Add type import from shared types or define locally

Title: AssetSignalConfig type missing
ExploitScenario: Component fails to compile
Remediation: Import from hooks/useAssetSignals or define locally

Title: Implicit any types in AssetConditionView
ExploitScenario: Type safety lost for signal mappings
Remediation: Add explicit type annotations

Title: Untracked temp files in working tree
ExploitScenario: Cluttered repo reduces maintainability
Remediation: Remove or gitignore temp files. Coder discipline: never commit debug scripts

Title: TODO in production path
ExploitScenario: Incomplete feature may cause silent data loss
Remediation: Implement or remove TODO

Title: Dual config paths in Edge v2 container
Evidence: Config read from /app/config/ but we patched /app/agent/config/
ExploitScenario: Configuration drift between expected and actual path
Remediation: Standardize to single config path. Document in code

Title: VPS git on unrelated commit
ExploitScenario: Cannot reproduce runtime state from source
Remediation: Docker image build and deployment not traceable to git
Owner: Reset VPS repo to main. Build from HEAD

Title: Edge v2 image 9 days stale
Evidence: Built 2026-07-13 running with docker cp hotfixes
ExploitScenario: Image not reproducible. Config changes lost on restart
Remediation: Rebuild image from main commit ae8b611

Title: Simulator port 9998 public
ExploitScenario: HTTP simulator accessible from internet exposing internal signal structure
Remediation: Restrict to 127.0.0.1 or remove

Title: Test server port 9999 public
ExploitScenario: Test HTTP server exposed to internet
Remediation: Restrict or stop service

Title: API key has no identity or endpoint scopes
Evidence: API key grants full access without principal mapping
ExploitScenario: Stolen API key grants unrestricted system access
Remediation: Add service identity principal scope and per-endpoint authorization

Title: JWT refresh does not preserve role
ExploitScenario: Admin session silently downgrades to operator after token refresh
Remediation: Pass payload.get(""role"") to create_access_token

Title: Credential material mixed with operational Edge config
Evidence: Passwords and session secrets stored in same YAML as operational config
ExploitScenario: Single file exposure leaks both credentials and system topology
Remediation: Separate secrets from config. Mount secrets as env vars or files

Title: Shared API key embedded in frontend JavaScript
ExploitScenario: Any visitor to PlantOS UI can extract the shared API key from browser devtools
Remediation: Remove API key fallback. Browser authenticates only as user

Title: require_admin function not defined
Evidence: Imported by users and edge_users routers but function does not exist
ExploitScenario: Users and edge_users routers crash on import
Remediation: Create require_admin FastAPI dependency in middleware/auth.py

Title: No mandatory CI status checks
Evidence: No GitHub Actions workflow enforces build/test/scan gates
ExploitScenario: PRs can merge with broken builds or exposed secrets
Remediation: Create .github/workflows/phase8-quality-gate.yml
